message: bd-n3wn: mark memoized reuse as incremental

files.patch:
@@ -453,6 +453,7 @@ {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: structural recommendations and remediation hints","description":"Goal Add structural recommendation UX that translates fnx findings into concrete user guidance. Scope - Suggest simplifications (bridge hotspots, articulation choke points, disconnected islands). - Provide recommendation categories with confidence and rationale. - Support CLI + wasm payloads for front-end display. Acceptance Criteria - Recommendations are stable and machine-readable. - Each recommendation includes rationale + suggested remediation. - E2E scenarios validate recommendations for representative anti-pattern graphs.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements): 1) Unit tests - Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules. 2) Integration tests - Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries. 3) End-to-end tests - Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead. - E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly. 4) Structured logging and evidence - Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields: `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`. - Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage. 5) Determinism and regression checks - Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries. - Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead. 6) Quality gates and build policy - Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes. - All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- ` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.334729935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:28:51.355492549Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","fnx","parser","ux","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:25:41.010443511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.457686835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.513701911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.334729935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:28:51.352909859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: user guide, examples, and troubleshooting","description":"Goal Ship a user-focused FNX integration guide with scenario-driven examples and decision playbooks. Scope - Add docs: when fnx helps, when to disable, and expected tradeoffs. - Include before/after examples with command lines and output excerpts. - Add troubleshooting section for projection mode confusion and perf concerns. Acceptance Criteria - Docs are self-contained and linked from README/CLI help. - Examples are reproducible via scripts checked into repo. - Documentation validation scripts run in CI.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements): 1) Unit tests - Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules. 2) Integration tests - Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries. 3) End-to-end tests - Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead. - E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly. 4) Structured logging and evidence - Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields: `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`. - Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage. 5) Determinism and regression checks - Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries. - Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead. 6) Quality gates and build policy - Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes. - All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- ` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.397625714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:25:40.788369921Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","docs","fnx","ux","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:23:22.963912257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.397625714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.707759512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.769122960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.831272040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-mskg","title":"Sequence: decode Mermaid entity escapes in note and message text","description":"# Sequence entity escape parity ## Goal Support Mermaid sequence note/message entity escapes like `#35;`, `#59;`, and named HTML entities in user-visible text. ## Scope - Decode decimal numeric and named HTML-style entities in sequence note text and message labels. - Preserve existing `
` line-break behavior. - Keep identifier parsing unchanged; this is display-text decoding, not ID rewriting. - Add focused parser and renderer coverage. ## Acceptance Criteria - Sequence message labels decode `#59;`, `#35;`, and representative named entities. - Sequence note text decodes the same entities and still respects `
` line breaks. - Workspace check/clippy/format gates pass via `rch` and `cargo fmt --check`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:32:53.270139630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:44.361044572Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:44.360571086Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-n3wn","title":"WASM layout summary marks memoized reuse as incremental","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T18:24:59.403958630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:27:00.978113384Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T18:27:00.977845957Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-nep0","title":"EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity","description":"# EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity ## Goal Achieve 100% feature parity with mermaid-js across ALL diagram types. Every syntax construct, visual feature, and rendering capability that mermaid-js supports must work in frankenmermaid — parser, layout, and renderers (SVG + terminal). ## Why This Matters frankenmermaid's value proposition is being a better alternative to mermaid-js. But 'better' is meaningless if users hit unsupported syntax. Every 'Partial' in the support table is a potential user abandonment. The bar is: any valid mermaid-js input must produce correct output in frankenmermaid. ## Scope This epic covers ALL 24+ diagram types. Each has a sub-epic with granular tasks for: 1. Parser completeness (every syntax construct) 2. Layout correctness (specialized algorithms where needed) 3. SVG rendering (every visual element) 4. Terminal rendering (best-effort representation) ## Success Criteria - Every diagram type moves from 'Partial' to 'Implemented' in the support table - The mermaid-js compatibility test suite (bd-2xl.5) passes at 100% - README and demo showcase reflect full support - No valid mermaid-js input produces a parse error or blank output","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:41:46.188949046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:22.243386220Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:22.243112522Z","close_reason":"All 23 deps closed. Full mermaid-js parity achieved across all diagram types.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-0pi0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:32.433199422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-1a45","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:34.193057308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-4gmf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:35.095329004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-54qp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:25.218886499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-68e5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:27.563546479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-6qxv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:29.969622861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-90m7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:24.432361365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-b6gg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:21.231409785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-ct6f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:28.362485999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-f4er","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:31.592587609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-f6ow","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:25.989409335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-i7e5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17 {"id":"bd-nep0.1","title":"Block Beta: add SVG family styling and hide placeholder space nodes","description":"# Block Beta SVG parity slice Goal: - Move Block Beta beyond generic node styling on the SVG surface. - Render block-beta nodes with family-specific styling and keep synthetic `space` placeholders invisible. Scope: - Add explicit Block Beta SVG node classes. - Apply family-specific fill/stroke/text styling. - Suppress synthetic `__space_*` fallback labels and make placeholder nodes visually invisible while preserving layout occupancy. - Add focused renderer tests. Verification: - `rch exec -- cargo test -p fm-render-svg block_beta -- --nocapture` - `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` - `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` - `cargo fmt --check --package fm-render-svg`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:15.230916251Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:15.230655437Z","close_reason":"Block-beta now supports classDef/style directives via generic extract_style_directives (bd-f4er). Space placeholder nodes get block-beta-space CSS class. Further visual polish is incremental.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block-beta","parity","renderer","svg"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nep0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nep0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-ooad","title":"Reject backslash/control chars in sanitize_style_value","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:15:48.238473936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:16:10.248767054Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:16:10.248386465Z","close_reason":"Hardened sanitize_style_value against backslash/control escapes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
